title: DISTINCT changes order of results
description:
Hi,
for the query below, the DISTINCT operator (try to leave that out) changes the result set, although the answer semantic should be the same:
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX res: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?uri
WHERE {
        ?x rdf:type dbo:Album .
        ?x dbo:artist res:Elvis_Presley .
        ?x dbo:releaseDate ?y .
        ?x dbo:recordLabel ?uri .
}
ORDER BY ASC(?y)
LIMIT 1

Obviously the DISTINCT operator is applied before the LIMIT operator but it changes the order. I am not sure whether this is a bug or a feature.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE Albums (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, artist TEXT, releaseDate TEXT, recordLabel TEXT);
INSERT INTO Albums (id, artist, releaseDate, recordLabel) VALUES (1, 'Elvis Presley', '1956-01-01', 'RCA'), (2, 'Elvis Presley', '1957-01-01', 'RCA'), (3, 'Elvis Presley', NULL, 'RCA');
SELECT recordLabel FROM Albums WHERE artist = 'Elvis Presley' AND releaseDate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY releaseDate ASC LIMIT 1;
SELECT recordLabel FROM Albums WHERE artist = 'Elvis Presley' ORDER BY releaseDate ASC LIMIT 1;
SELECT recordLabel FROM Albums WHERE artist = 'Elvis Presley' AND (releaseDate IS NULL OR releaseDate < '1957-01-01') ORDER BY releaseDate ASC LIMIT 1;
key_bug_features: DISTINCT operator changes result order unexpectedly
